title: Workflow — real near-miss with monitoring data and close-call evidence
code_snippet:
# User provides: Grafana alert showing Redis connection pool hit 95% capacity
# at 03:22 UTC during traffic spike. Auto-scaler kicked in at 03:24 and
# resolved before customer impact. PagerDuty alert fired but auto-resolved.
# Logs show 3 connection timeouts (internal retries succeeded). No customer
# errors, no SLO budget consumed.
#
# User says: 'We almost had another Redis incident last night. Pool hit 95%
# and auto-scaler barely saved us. Can you write up a near-miss analysis?
# Here are the Grafana alerts and logs.'
expected_feedback: Workflow: Draft mode + Quick depth (SEV-4 near-miss, caught before customer impact). (1) Gate 1 passes: incident data present (alerts, logs, metrics). (2) Classify as SEV-4 Informational — near-miss, no customer impact. (3) Timeline: construct from Grafana + PagerDuty + logs, even though brief. (4) Root cause: apply 5-Why — why did pool approach exhaustion? Same root cause class as actual incidents. (5) Near-miss framing: treat with same rigor as real incident per rca-techniques.md — a near-miss has the same root cause as an actual incident. (6) Action items: prevent the near-miss from becoming an actual incident next time. (7) Brief write-up appropriate per SEV-4 requirements.
